Honestly shocked by how bad the cleanliness was here. Stained sheets, mold in the shower, hair everywhere. We tried the pool but it was green and the gym smelled like mildew — the amenities are a joke. Asked the front desk for help three times and the service was just shrugs and empty promises.

We booked this place for a family reunion and it nearly ruined the trip. Grime on every surface, cockroaches near the bathroom drain — the cleanliness is nonexistent. Tried to use the so-called amenities but the pool was closed and the restaurant gave two of us food poisoning. Worst service I've ever encountered; nobody cared at all.

Where do I even start. Room 304 had crumbs in the bed from the last guest and the bathroom hadn't been touched — zero cleanliness standards. Went to check out the amenities and the fitness center was padlocked, the hot tub was drained, and the business center had one broken PC. Called for help and the service was hostile. Actual hostility.

I travel for work 200 nights a year and this is the worst hotel I've stayed in. The cleanliness was appalling — dusty vents blowing stale air, sticky floors, bathroom mirror covered in smudges. The amenities were equally bad with a tiny gym, broken WiFi, and a restaurant that ran out of food by 7pm. Reported issues to Marco at the desk and the service was dismissive at every turn.

Checked in late on a Thursday and knew immediately this was a mistake. Our room reeked of cigarettes and the cleanliness was atrocious — stained carpet, grimy remote, soap scum everywhere. We tried the amenities but the spa was closed indefinitely and the pool had debris floating in it. Asked for a room change and the service we got was an eye-roll and a 'we'll see what we can do' that went nowhere.

Just awful. Not an ounce of cleanliness in this entire building — the lobby carpet is stained, the hallways smell, and our room had visible dirt on the headboard. Every amenity was a letdown: broken gym equipment, a freezing pool, and a gift shop that was unstaffed. The service from check-in to checkout was rude, slow, and completely indifferent.

Brought my kids here for spring break and I'm still mad about it. Found used tissues behind the toilet and the cleanliness of the bathroom was genuinely alarming. We couldn't even distract ourselves because every one of the amenities was broken or closed — pool, gym, all of it. When I complained, the service was a half-hearted apology and nothing else.

I don't understand how this place stays open. The cleanliness borders on a health hazard — mildew on the shower curtain, hair in the drain, towels that smelled sour. The amenities are false advertising: the 'spa' is a locked door, the 'restaurant' serves microwaved leftovers. And honestly the service was the worst part, which is saying something. Staff couldn't care less.

Stayed here for a conference. Dust bunnies under the bed, grimy bathroom fixtures, stained carpet. Tried using the business center for a presentation but the projector was broken. Honestly just a terrible hotel all around, would not come back.

Four nights of pure misery. Not a single aspect of cleanliness was acceptable — from the stained bedsheets to the sticky bathroom floor to the overflowing trash in the hallways. The pool was a cloudy mess, the gym had rusted weights, and the restaurant's food was inedible, so the amenities were worthless. My wife asked Tina at the front desk for fresh towels and the service she received was a blank stare.

Disgusting room, period. The cleanliness was the worst I've seen in twenty years of traveling — black mold in the shower, mysterious spots on the carpet, and a bathtub ring that could've been there for months. The so-called amenities were no escape either; the pool was closed and the gym door was jammed shut. I've never felt so unwelcome.

We specifically chose this hotel for the pool and spa. What a mistake — the amenities were in total disrepair. Pool water was cloudy, the sauna was out of order, and the fitness center had two working machines. I expected rough rooms but even the cleanliness in our 'renovated' suite was terrible. Dust everywhere and the bathroom smelled musty.

Room was filthy. No other word for it. We found the previous guest's razor on the bathroom shelf, the sheets had marks, and the cleanliness was just... nonexistent. Called housekeeping four times. The service was equally terrible — they never came, never called back, never acknowledged the problem. We checked out a day early.

Our anniversary trip, completely ruined. The cleanliness was so bad we slept in our clothes the first night — I couldn't trust the sheets. Reported it to the front desk and the service was just a tired 'sorry about that' with no follow-up. No room change, no compensation, nothing. Still angry weeks later.

Honestly the amenities are what you'd expect from a place that charges half this rate. Broken lounge chairs by the pool, a gym that's more of a closet, and a restaurant where even the coffee was bad. Plus the elevator was out of service two of our three days. Complete waste of money.
Terrible amenities across the board. Freezing pool, WiFi that barely worked, and a restaurant with three items on the menu. We spent all our time off-property just to avoid the place.

Can't get over how dirty this place was. The cleanliness was rock-bottom — cobwebs, grime in the corners, a bathroom floor that was sticky to walk on. We asked housekeeping to come back and re-clean and they simply didn't. That was our entire experience with this hotel in a nutshell.
Stained pillowcases, mildew on the shower tiles, exhaust fan pushing stale air. I don't usually leave reviews but the cleanliness here was so bad I felt obligated. Avoid.

Broken hot tub, torn gym cushions, empty vending machines. We spent our whole trip at nearby restaurants because the on-site amenities were unbearable. Front desk staff were either absent or shrugging whenever we asked for help.

Wanted to like this place because of the location but everything else was a disaster. Noisy room, shot mattress, scuff marks on the walls. Only positive is the parking lot was easy to navigate. That's literally it.
